text: STRATEGY QUESTION from the boss (he is FRUSTRATED — ~$1000 spent, $0 revenue so far, so be HONEST/grounded, NO hype, NO 画大饼). Question: Should he open a SHOPIFY store? Specifically: (1) Dropshipping / reselling OTHER people's goods with NO inventory — can it actually be profitable in 2026, honestly? (2) How does it differ from our existing model (ShotPro = a software product) and from the Amazon Associate/affiliate program? (3) He is in NYC and can source some CHEAP, decent-quality goods locally — feasibility of listing those on Shopify? Give a SHORT, concrete, honest answer: real margins/effort, the #1 bottleneck, and your single recommendation. Reply via bin/warroom-say (under 900 chars). He wants a round of war-room opinions. Just answer via bin/warroom-say, no files needed.
